arts.ttl: Matchday Guide: Aston Villa v Ipswich Town
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Ipswich Town - Sat, Feb 15th 2025, 15:00 Aston Villa return to Premier League action on Saturday afternoon, as Ipswich Town visit Villa Park (ko 3pm). Unai Emery's side will be looking to maintain their strong home form in B6 while recording back-to-back wins, after securing their spot in the fifth round of the FA Cup with a 2-1 win over Tottenham Hotspur last weekend. Here's everything you need to know ahead of the encounter to enjoy your visit on matchday... Getting to the game Click on the sections below to learn more about tickets, Villa Park and transport to the stadium. Mobile Tickets Villa Park is now fully NFC ticketing enabled for supporters to access the stadium. Near-Field Communication (NFC) enables fans to enter the stadium via Apple or Google Wallet. Supporters can now also access their match-by-match tickets and enter the stadium directly via the Aston Villa app. Simply log into your Digital Account in the app and your tickets will appear under the ‘Tickets’ section. Supporters must update their app to the latest version to ensure in-app ticketing works without issue. Tickets A limited number of general admission tickets for the match remain. Book online here. The best way to secure tickets for future matches at Villa Park is via a Membership. Get Matchday Ready Ahead of the match at Villa Park, the club would like to advise supporters of the following ticketing information to ensure everyone enjoys their visit to our stadium. Fans will need to provide one of the two specified ticket types to gain entry to the stadium - access tickets in our official Aston Villa mobile app or download to Apple/Google Wallet for NFC entry. Hospitality and Premium Experiences The club has a range of new hospitality and GA+ areas to complement our existing offer. Check out all the information and book online here. Hospitality bookings for our upcoming home matches can be made over the phone on 0333 323 1982 (option 1) or by emailing hospitality@avfc.co.uk. Under-3s Policy Many supporters may not be aware of the policy regarding under-3s, so we are reminding supporters that under-3s are only permitted to be in the Family areas in the Trinity Road stand at Villa Park and must have a match ticket. Further information can be found here Aston Villa Ticketing (avfc.co.uk) Visiting Villa Park It's important that supporters take steps to plan their visit to the stadium. Turnstiles open at 1.30pm, so make sure you arrive at the stadium in plenty of time ahead of kick-off to ensure smooth entry. Please note, access to stiles is maintained during the first half and will close once the half-time whistle is blown. However you're planning on getting to the match, take a look at our Travel & Parking guide before leaving home. A traffic exclusion zone will be in operation around the stadium, meaning there will be less traffic on Trinity Road and Witton Lane from up to two hours before the match starts. The Holte Pub will open three hours prior to kick-off and close two hours after full-time. Parking and Travel Supporters are advised that the supply of car parking around Villa Park is limited. Fans can pre-book their space via either of the links below: www.astonvillaparking.co.uk/football/events www.countrywidemanagementsolutions.co.uk We will continue to work with local parking operators to offer fans the opportunity to book parking near the stadium. We would strongly encourage all supporters who have not pre-booked their car parking to travel to Villa Park by alternative modes. Covered cycle parking is available at our Inner City Academy on Witton Road. Plan ahead and check train timetables for direct services or travel earlier for less busy services. Visit Transport for West Midlands’ Journey Planner or the National Rail Enquiries real-time journey planner here. There are a number of Park and Ride sites across the region, for further details visit Park and Ride. To help you plan your journey by public transport, visit How to get to Villa Park. Birmingham city centre and train services calling at the Poundland Bescot Stadium are expected to be busy on Saturday, with other local football teams in action at the same time. There is no planned disruption to key road networks to Villa Park on Saturday. Traffic signals have been optimised around Villa Park to help improve flow of traffic away from the ground. This should assist with journey times for our AV82 city centre shuttle bus (see below). City Centre and Tamworth Shuttle Buses Aston Villa is delighted to confirm to supporters that our AV82 city centre shuttle bus service will continue to run on matchdays this season. This follows a successful trial of the service at the back end of last season. The service will be in operation for all men’s home matches in the Premier League, UEFA Champions League and all domestic cup competitions, helping supporters travel more easily to and from Villa Park. Run by Stagecoach, the AV82 shuttle picks supporters up from outside Birmingham New Street Station on Dudley Street, running directly to Villa Park. Buses run every 15 minutes, starting three hours before kick-off for every home fixture. Services run back to Birmingham New Street at regular intervals for up to 90 minutes after the final whistle of every game, with services departing from Grosvenor Road, on the walking route towards Aston Station. Traffic signals have been optimised around Villa Park to help improve flow of traffic away from the ground. This should assist with journey times for our AV82 city centre shuttle bus. The club also has a dedicated bus service, AV1, that runs to Villa Park from Tamworth on matchday. You can purchase tickets here. More information is available here. Cycle Parking Covered and secure cycle parking is available a short walk away from Villa Park at our Inner City Academy, Brook Vale, on Witton Road. Accessibility information Supporters with accessibility requirements travelling to Villa Park by rail are advised to look out for the accessibility signage on both platforms at Witton Station. Station staff will be able to help you if you wait in the designated areas. More information is available here. For any supporters with additional mobility needs who have questions about travel to and from Villa Park on a matchday, please contact accessibility@avfc.co.uk. Pre-match activities Click on the sections below to learn more about activities taking place at Villa Park before kick-off. Enjoy the Fan Zone Villa Park's new Fan Zone is again open on Saturday, located in the former Players' Car Park. The new space opens its doors at 12.00pm, with supporters able to enjoy a bar area and food. The Fan Zone is also in prime position to watch player arrivals from 1.30pm. We also have entertainment taking place on the stage opposite Trinity Reception, including live music from Quadrox. We want to see your experience of matchday at Villa Park! Post your photos and tag #UTV and you could be featured on the big screens at the stadium! Supporters are advised that the R44 accessible entrance will be closed for a period of time. Family Fun Zone Work is soon to begin on the transformation of the existing Academy building at Villa Park. Unfortunately, due to the planned works, the Academy building is not operational to the extent to which we can safely open the Family Fun Zone. The club are working on alternate arrangements to enable a continuation of some of the family-friendly pre-match activities. Updates will follow in the coming weeks. Food and Beverage Villa Park offers a wide range of cuisine and beverages on a matchday. Outside the stadium, several street food trucks will sit alongside a bar serving alcoholic and soft drinks from 1.30pm. Supporters are reminded that Aston Villa adopts a one-cup system at Villa Park, in our continued environmental sustainability efforts. Cashless Stadium Please note, Villa Park will be cashless to help ensure safe social distancing practices and minimise contact at all points of sale. This includes the Villa Store, with card payments widely accepted in and around the stadium. The only exception is programme kiosks outside the ground, which will still accept cash. In-stadia betting kiosks will also be in operation for this fixture and such service will be provided on a cashless basis. North Grounds Update Aston Villa Football Club has announced its plans for a bold regeneration project at Villa Park that will build on the Club’s 150-year heritage, while also creating the fan experience zone of the future. Expected to go live Christmas 2025, the new project includes the launch of The Warehouse, a new multi-use venue set to become a centrepiece of the matchday experience and a hub for year-round entertainment, along with a new ticketing office, improved plaza in the North Grounds, and expanded footprint of the club shop. Admission to The Warehouse will be free for ticketed supporters on matchdays, when the venue becomes the largest beer hall in the Premier League – a vibrant space to connect, enjoy a pint, and soak in pre-match excitement with state-of-the-art LED lights and flat-screen TVs. Beyond matchdays, The Warehouse will transform into the Midlands’ premier concert venue and event space. The 3,500-seater venue will host world-class performances, from live music and sporting events to community activities, creating a versatile space for audiences of all kinds. Designed to bring the Aston and Birmingham communities together, it will also serve as a platform for local and global talent, making an impact far beyond Villa Park. More information here. Matchday Programme The latest edition of the Villa News & Record will be available to purchase at Saturday's match! Fans can continue their sticker album collection, while the programme is also packed with features and news from around the club. Supporters will be able to purchase copies from sellers at Villa Park on matchday, while they're also available to purchase in the club shop and online here. Villa Store Click on the sections below to learn about merchandise points around the stadium. Store Opening Hours There are a number of places where supporters can shop for Aston Villa merchandise on a matchday, including the upgraded Villa Store. Below you'll find the opening times for each shop around the ground... Villa Store: 9am-5.30pm Holte Store: 12pm-3pm and 4.45pm-5.30pm 82 Lounge: 3pm-5.30pm Lower Grounds: 3pm-5.30pm Trinity Store: 1.30pm-3pm and at half-time 50% off third kit! Those visiting Villa Park on Saturday will be able to take advantage of an incredible offer of 50% off 2024/25 third kit items! This fantastic deal is redeemable IN STORE ONLY at the above locations, and only for today's match, so make sure you head along to a retail outlet in B6 to make the most of the opportunity. During the game Click on the sections below to learn more about events that take place during the match. See it...hear it...report it! If you witness discriminatory behaviour at a match or around a stadium, please report it to the police or a steward. Supporters at Villa Park can text REPORT to 60060 along with the location of the incident and details of what you have witnessed. The text line also includes the reporting of any safeguarding concerns. The text service will be supported by our security team and West Midlands Police. Each text costs one standard message at your network rate. The club may share your details with West Midlands Police if further information is required to assist with a criminal prosecution. All-seater Stadium We would like to remind supporters that Villa Park is an all-seater stadium. In all areas of Villa Park, we have children, elderly fans and those with medical conditions who are unable to see or watch the match if supporters stand up in front of them. Please be mindful of this at all times and thank you for your cooperation. Fan Engagement Click the sections below to learn about how you can get involved on matchday and our fan loyalty programme, Villa Rewards. Get Involved New for 2024/25, the ‘Villa Predictor’ game will give fans the opportunity to win a £500 store voucher if they correctly predict the outcome of the game. T&Cs apply. We want to know about your matchday experience at Villa Park. Tweet us using #UTV and you could feature on the big screens at the stadium! Pride Rewards The Pride Rewards Portal is back up and running. Log in here. AV150 Legacy Wall Aston Villa is excited to reveal a new AV150 Legacy Wall at Villa Park as part of our 150th anniversary celebrations. Supporters have the opportunity to become part of our iconic home, wherever you are in the world, by purchasing your very own personalised plaque at Villa Park. And phase one of the installation of the Legacy Wall is closing soon, as supporters have until 11pm on matchday to secure a plaque, available from £80, and be guaranteed to have it in place at Villa Park during our anniversary season. Our Legacy Wall is a place where you, your friends and family can show your support for the club, celebrate births, marriages, anniversaries or pay tribute to loved ones. For more information, click here.

arts.ttl: Liam Delap issues transfer update as Chelsea eye HUGE summer deal
arts.sum: Liam Delap has insisted that he remains happy at Ipswich Town, despite significant transfer interest from Chelsea . Chelsea, however, are currently leading the race for his signature and Enzo Maresca’s side are expected to ramp up negotiations for Delap shortly as they look to sign a new centre forward this summer.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa handed Marcus Rashford boost as Ruben Amorim makes Man United transfer admission
arts.sum: Ruben Amorim has issued a stark warning that Manchester United will need to offload players before making any fresh signings this summer due to the financial constraints currently affecting several aspects of the club. The admission comes as United have several high profile players out on loan with other clubs, not least Marcus Rashford at Aston Villa where there is an option to buy the striker in place at the end of the season.

arts.ttl: Aston Villa vs Liverpool match request rejected by Premier League as club left 'frustrated'
arts.sum: Aston Villa and Liverpool are due to face off during a busy February for both clubs, though Unai Emery and Arne Slot will both enjoy a quieter time of things in March

arts.ttl: Unai Emery sends message to Aston Villa players over summer transfer window
arts.sum: Aston Villa manager Unai Emery signed five players in the winter window and made up to £100m in player sales Unai Emery believes his Aston Villa squad is stronger and has more balance than before the club entered the winter window.

arts.ttl: Unai Emery SLAMS 'unfair' Premier League fixture list as Aston Villa prepare for five games in 14 days after Liverpool clash was rescheduled
arts.sum: TOM COLLOMOSSE: Unai Emery has branded the Premier League fixture list 'unfair' after Aston Villa's clash with Liverpool was rescheduled for next week.

arts.ttl: Furious Unai Emery blasts 'not fair' Aston Villa decision and says 'it is not our job'
arts.sum: Furious Unai Emery blasts 'not fair' Aston Villa decision and says 'it is not our job' Aston Villa will play five games in all competitions in 13 days, but only one Premier League match next month

arts.ttl: Aston Villa left 'frustrated' after Premier League reject Liverpool fixture request
arts.sum: Liverpool's visit to Villa Park was initially scheduled to take place on Saturday, March 15, but was moved to Wednesday, February 19 after it clashed with the Carabao Cup final weekend, where Arne Slot's side will face Newcastle United. Aston Villa have been left frustrated after the Premier League reportedly refused to move next week's clash with Liverpool to a later date.

arts.ttl: Team News: Aston Villa vs. Ipswich Town injury, suspension list, predicted XIs
arts.sum: Sports Mole rounds up all of the latest injury and suspension news ahead of Saturday's Premier League clash between Aston Villa and Ipswich Town.

arts.ttl: Liverpool suffer fresh injury scare before Aston Villa as striker misses training
arts.sum: Liverpool manager Arne Slot has confirmed that forward Cody Gakpo is a doubt to take on Wolverhampton Wanderers this weekend - and he's in a race against time to be available to travel south to Birmingham and face Aston Villa on Wednesday night. I had to take him off because of an injury, because he got a knock, so let's see how he is today," Slot replied, when asked of the situation regarding Gakpo.
